description_long: This structure is a traditional trullo, characterized by its circular stone base and distinctive double conical roof. The walls are constructed from irregular, roughly-hewn stones, giving the exterior a rustic and robust appearance. The roof is made of overlapping gray stone tiles, forming two stacked conical sections, with the upper cone being smaller and topped by a decorative pinnacle. Moss and grass are visible at the base, suggesting age and integration with the surrounding environment. One side features a small, wooden door set into the stone wall, typical of trullo entrances. The overall look is that of a historic rural dwelling from the Apulian region of Italy.
description: A circular stone trullo with a double conical gray-tiled roof and a wooden door, topped by a decorative pinnacle. The structure exudes rustic charm and traditional Apulian architectural style.
description_view_0: This view highlights the layered conical roof and the rough stonework of the walls, with the pinnacle clearly visible.
description_view_1: The roof's overlapping stone tiles and the circular wall are prominent, with the pinnacle centered and no door visible.
description_view_2: The conical roof and stone wall are in focus, showing the even distribution of stonework and the mossy base.
description_view_3: This view reveals the small wooden door set into the stone wall, distinguishing it as the likely entrance.
